mcqs.question: What is the primary purpose of the ANOVA technique?

options.text: To determine the average value of a dataset
To compare means among three or more groups
To identify the relationship between variables
To test a hypothesis about a population mean

mcqs.explanation: ANOVA (Analysis of Variance) is used to compare means among three or more groups to see if at least one of the groups is different from the others.

mcqs.question: Which type of ANOVA is used to compare means among three or more groups based on a single factor?

options.text: One-way ANOVA
Two-way ANOVA
Latin-square Design
Analysis of Co-Variance

mcqs.explanation: One-way ANOVA is used to compare means among three or more groups when there is only one independent variable or factor.

mcqs.question: What is the main advantage of using Latin-square Design?

options.text: It allows for the comparison of means among three or more groups
It reduces the risk of Type I error
It increases the precision of the estimates
It allows for the estimation of the variance components

mcqs.explanation: Latin-square Design is primarily used to control for two sources of variation simultaneously, increasing the precision of estimates by reducing error variance.

mcqs.question: What is Analysis of Co-Variance (ANCOVA)?

options.text: A method for comparing means among three or more groups
A method for estimating the variance components
A method for adjusting group means for the effect of a continuous covariate
A method for testing a hypothesis about a population mean

mcqs.explanation: Analysis of Co-Variance is a method used to adjust the group means for the effect of one or more continuous covariates, increasing precision by accounting for extraneous variation.

mcqs.question: In the basic principle of ANOVA, the total sum of squares (SST) is partitioned into which two main components?

options.text: Between-groups sum of squares (SSB) and Error sum of squares (SSE)
Regression sum of squares and Residual sum of squares
Treatment sum of squares and Block sum of squares
Main effect sum of squares and Interaction sum of squares

mcqs.explanation: In one-way ANOVA, SST = SSB (between groups) + SSW (within groups, also called error sum of squares SSE). This partitioning is fundamental to the technique.

mcqs.question: What does the F-statistic in ANOVA compare?

options.text: The variance between groups to the variance within groups
The sample mean to the population mean
The treatment effect to the block effect
The regression slope to zero

mcqs.explanation: The F-statistic is calculated as MSB/MSW (Mean Square Between / Mean Square Within). A large F-value suggests the between-group variance is significantly larger than the within-group variance, indicating group means differ.

mcqs.question: In a one-way ANOVA with 4 groups and 10 observations per group, what are the degrees of freedom for the between-groups variation?
mcqs.explanation: Degrees of freedom between groups (dfB) = number of groups (k) - 1. Here, k=4, so dfB = 4-1 = 3.
